The author was at a restaurant with her family. The man with a dirty face opened the door for them. The kids made a comment about his smell, so the mom explained to look beyond the dirt. Later when she went back to the car for the car-sick pills, the doorman was opening the door again, and she said a loud thank you. Then she ordered food for him, explaining to her kids, and he was grateful. The question is about the purpose of her saying a loud thank you. The options are A through D. Let's look at each option: A. She wanted to give the children a lesson of gratitude. B. She showed her real love for him. C. She wanted to give the doorman a hand. D. She just showed her polite manner. In the passage, the author mentions that she wanted to set a good example for her children. When she said the thank you, she had just explained to her kids about looking beyond the dirt, and then later ordered food for him.
